sections.paragraph:
an unused root, Arab. ساحل to peal off, to shell, to scale, whence שְׁחֵלֶת. To this answer in the Indo-Germanic languages, σκῦλον, Germ. Schale, fchälen, Engl. to scale.

II.

שחל

perhaps, to roar, as a lion. Compare Arab. ساحل to bray, as an ass, ساُحاَلُ vox in pectore reciprocata, the braying of an ass. Jeuhari and Kam.; cognate roots are הָלַל, צָלַל, German, hallen, gellen, fchallen (Notk. fkellen). Hence-

שחל

m. a lion, a poetic word, Job 4:10 10:16 28:8 Psa 91:13 Pro 26:13 Hos 5:14 13:7 . Bochart (Hieroz.) understands the blackish lion of Syria (Plin. H. N., viii. 17). Compare שָׁחֹר, the letters ל and ר being interchanged.
